title: dianthus alpinus subsp. repens
intro: Dianthus alpinus subsp. repens, also known as Alpine Pink or Creeping Pink, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dianthus alpinus subsp. repens include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight against infections, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and topical applications, which can be used to treat various skin conditions and wounds.
